concerns.text: Task T024 instructs re-calculating network statistics on 'existing permuted correlation matrices' for the threshold sweep. This violates Spec US-3 and FR-005 which mandate re-running the permutation engine for each threshold. The null distribution of 'edge density' is fundamentally dependent on the threshold; reusing a single null distribution generated at one threshold (or without thresholding) to evaluate significance at other thresholds is statistically invalid and weakens the FR-005 requirement.

Task T016b mandates a 'single-run validation' of the synthetic dataset. This violates Spec US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3 and FR-009, which require confirming the observed statistic falls within the central [deferred] of the null distribution (p > 0.05) for 'at least 95% of runs' (implying a meta-analysis of multiple runs to verify the false positive rate). A single run cannot statistically validate the 95% pass rate requirement.

Task T061 implements a 'Runtime Feasibility Gate' that logs a warning but explicitly forbids automatic reduction of the permutation count (N=1000). This violates Spec Assumptions and SC-004, which state that if runtime exceeds the limit, the permutation count 'will be reduced as a fallback'. The task implements a blocking warning instead of the required adaptive fallback mechanism, weakening the SC-004 constraint.

Task T064 requires 'Manually verify the exact raw download URLs' and update config.py. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which mandates that external datasets be fetched from the same canonical source on every run without manual intervention. Introducing a manual human step into an automated research pipeline breaks the reproducibility constraint.

Task T004 defines the 6 specific UCI datasets with 'verified download URLs' but leaves the URL fields empty (e.g., 'Wine: ` `'). This violates Spec FR-001 which requires the system to download these specific datasets. A task that defines a requirement but leaves the critical data source (URL) undefined fails to satisfy the FR-001 constraint for implementation.
